attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon races across the land. It is said that north winds will somehow blow whenever it appears.

attacks.name: Inferno Spear
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy and a Lightning Energy attached to Rayquaza.

flavorText: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land while barking a cry that sounds like crashing thunder.

attacks.name: Wormhole
attacks.effect: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Destructive Tsunami
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

attacks.name: Scorching Wing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

attacks.name: Volcano Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

attacks.name: Wild Blaze
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

attacks.name: Time Rewind
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.

attacks.name: Cell Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Psychic Energy attached to Deoxys and remove 6 damage counters from Deoxys.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this Effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't Darkness. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose any 2 cards you find there and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put as many of them as you like back on top of your deck in any order. Then, put the remaining cards on the bottom of your deck in any order.

fullDisplayName: Professor Oak's New Theory 83/95 Call of Legends
rules:
You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

fullDisplayName: Professor Elm's Training Method 82/95 Call of Legends
rules:
You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, if that player's opponent has 6 or more Pokémon in the Lost Zone, the player may choose to win the game.
Put 1 Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Vulpix.

flavorText: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to Totodile. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

attacks.name: Fake Tears
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn, and any damage done to Teddiursa by attack is reduced by 30 (after applying weakness and resistance).

flavorText: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

attacks.name: Nutritional Support
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

attacks.name: Whismy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

attacks.name: Prehistoric Wisdom
attacks.effect: Choose a card from your hand and put it in the Lost Zone. Then, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Relicanth by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Discovered by chance during deep-sea explorations, it has not changed since ancient times.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Pineco's Surprise Attack attack's base damage is 80.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

attacks.name: Messenger
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle Pidgey and all cards attached to it back into your deck.

flavorText: Common in grassy areas and forests, it is very docile and will chase off enemies by flapping up sand.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: As a sign of affection, it bumps with its snout. However, it is so strong, it may send you flying.

attacks.name: Dual Draw
attacks.effect: Each player draws 3 cards.

flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

attacks.name: Selfish Draw
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put it into your hand. If not, discard it and draw a card.

attacks.name: Destructive Jaw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.
Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.
It dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.
For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.
If it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.
It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Cyndaquil.

flavorText: It is timid, and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn ,any damage done to Clefairy by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Remove 1 damage counter from Chikorita.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Chikorita does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sunrays.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from Vaporeon.

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

attacks.name: Poison Buildup
attacks.effect: Seviper is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Effect
attacks.effect: If Seviper is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage and remove the Special Condition Poisoned from Seviper.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

attacks.name: Tumble Over
attacks.effect: Riolu can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to Quilava.

flavorText: This Pokémon is fully covered by nonflammable fur. It can withstand any kind of fire attack.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It renders its prey immobile using well-developed claws, then carries the prey more than 60 miles to its nest.

attacks.name: Sleepy Lost
attacks.effect: Put the top card of your opponent's deck in the Lost Zone. Mime Jr. is now Asleep.

flavorText: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy's movements. Then it wastes no time in making itself scarce!

attacks.name: Play with Fire
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned. Magby is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is found in volcanic craters. Its body temperature is over 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit, so don't underestimate it.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Jolteon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to Flareon.

flavorText: It stores some of the air it inhales in its internal flame pouch, which heats it to over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.
A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Zangoose's Lost Claw attack's base damage is 80.

attacks.name: Lost Claw
attacks.effect: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and put it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its fur would all stand on end if it smelled a Seviper nearby. Its sharp claws tear up its foes.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Super Explosion
attacks.effect: Weezing does 90 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness to this damage.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailing finds all food buried deep underground.

attacks.name: Mischievous Punch
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. Tyrogue is now Asleep.

flavorText: Even though it is small, it can't be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to Typhlosion.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of Energy attached to Tangrowth.

attacks.name: Plow Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, put 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can't even see its eyes.

attacks.name: Layabout
attacks.effect: Remove all damage counters from Snorlax. Snorlax can't use Layabout during your next turn.

attacks.name: Clomp Clomp Clobber
attacks.effect: Put 1 Energy card attached to Snorlax in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its stomach's digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. It can even eat things off the ground.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

attacks.name: Steel Coat
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Metal Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

attacks.name: Headwind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the attack cost of each of the Defending Pokémon's attacks is ColorlessColorless more.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: It spreads its beautiful wings wide to frighten its enemies. It can fly at Mach 2 speed.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its fingertips emit a peculiar force field that hardens air to create an actual wall.

attacks.name: Sleeping Spell
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.effect: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from Meganium.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

attacks.name: Timid Tackle
attacks.effect: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

flavorText: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Acceleration Bolt
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Ampharos does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

attacks.name: Moonlight Fang
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Umbreon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon than has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

attacks.name: Inferno Spear
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy and a Lightning Energy attached to Rayquaza.

flavorText: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

attacks.name: Wormhole
attacks.effect: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

attacks.name: Shocking Bolt
attacks.effect: Put all Energy cards attached to Pachirisu in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It's one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.
Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

attacks.name: Hard Crush
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Mantle Bazooka
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Magmortar.

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Dimension Sphere
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each of your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras. Even foes it could not otherwise see.

attacks.name: Miasma Wind
attacks.effect: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Conditions affecting the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sooting Scent
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you'll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

attacks.name: Destructive Tsunami
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

attacks.name: Time Hollow
attacks.effect: Choose a number of your opponent's Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Jirachi. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of those Pokémon and put those cards back into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.

attacks.name: Fire Counterattack
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Fighting Pokémon in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dark Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

attacks.name: Scorching Wing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Close Combat
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Hitmontop by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.name: Volcano Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Everyone Explode Now
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage times the number of Pineco and Forretress you have in play. This attack does 30 damage to each of your Pineco and Forretress in play.

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

attacks.name: Solar Suggestion
attacks.effect: Move up to 4 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy's actions.

attacks.name: Time Rewind
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.

attacks.name: Cell Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Psychic Energy attached to Deoxys and remove 6 damage counters from Deoxys.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

attacks.name: Fairy Power
attacks.effect: Return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it in your hand.

flavorText: With its acute hearing, it can pick up sounds from far away. It usually hides in quiet places.
